free_text: Cross Section Measurements of Two-Nucleon Pickup
Reactions in the 13-C+D System at Deuteron Energies
Below 350 keV
Center For Applied Physical Sciences and
Department of Physics
350 K-V Accelerator
Abstract:  the Excitation Functions of 13-C(D,A0)11-B
And 13-C(D,A1)11-B Reactions with 5.167 and 3.307 MeV
Q-Values, Respectively, Exhibit Smooth Variations of
Cross Section with Energy.  The Angular Distribution of
13-C(D,A0)11-B Reaction is Asymmetric.  With Decreasing
Deuteron  Energy, the Degree of Asymmetry Increases and
Below 220 KeV Deuteron Energy the Angular  Distribution
Is Backward-Peaked.  This Indicates a Dominating
Contribution from the Direct Reaction in this Channel
Below 220 KeV Deuteron Energy.  The Angular
Distribution of 13-C(D,A1)11-B Reactions are Highly
Symmetric About 90 Degree, Indicating a Dominating
Compound Nucleus Contribution Over this Energy Range.
- By Compiler: Energy of First Excited Level of B-11
Was Taken from ENSDF by Compiler.
30 Micro-Gramm/Cm**2 Thick Enriched 13-C Foils.
Correction in sub.002, MeV---to keV,
according N.Otsuka remarks. Correction in Err-Analys,
according new Exfor rules.
By CAJAD-Figure 2,4 of J,AUJ,51,903,1998
Statistical uncertainties were 2-10 % for ground state
production and 5-6% for first excited level.
The systematic uncertainties, which were due to
charge integration, target thickness and the finite
angular resolution, were estimated to be 4-5%.
.Digitizing error of energy.
.The uncertainty is reported by authors on
figures.
By CAJAD-Figure 3,5 of J,AUJ,51,903,1998
.Digitizing error of angle
.The uncertainty is reported by authors on
figures.
Statistical uncertainties were 2-24 % for ground state
production and 2-8% for first excited level.
The systematic uncertainties, which were due to
charge integration, target thickness and the finite
angular resolution, were estimated to be 4-5%.
.Table of J,AUJ,51,903,1998
.The Uncertainties were Calculated from the
